questions.questionText:
A pie chart represents the sales of 180 items in four categories.

| Category | Angle |
|----------|-------|
| Books    | 80°   |
| Music    | 60°   |
| Games    | 100°  |
| Other    |  ?    |

(a) Find the angle for the 'Other' category.
(b) Find the number of Games sold.
(c) Find the fraction of items that were Books. Give your answer in its simplest form.

questions.context:
A frequency polygon is drawn to show the distances (km) cycled by members of a club during one week.

| Distance (km) | Frequency |
|---------------|----------|
| 0 < d ≤ 20   | 5        |
| 20 < d ≤ 40  | 12       |
| 40 < d ≤ 60  | 18       |
| 60 < d ≤ 80  | 10       |
| 80 < d ≤ 100 | 5        |

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Write down the midpoints used to draw the frequency polygon.

markScheme.desc: All five midpoints correct: 10, 30, 50, 70, 90 (B1 for at least three correct)

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Calculate an estimate of the mean distance cycled.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Explain one advantage of using a frequency polygon instead of a bar chart to display this data.
parts.correctAnswer: A frequency polygon allows easy comparison of two or more distributions on the same diagram

markScheme.desc: Easier to compare two (or more) distributions / shows trend / continuous data displayed more naturally oe

parts.label: d
parts.questionText: Show that the class containing the median is 40 < d ≤ 60.

questions.questionText:
Two groups of students sat the same test.

Group X: mean = 58, standard deviation not given, range = 30, n = 25
Group Y: mean = 62, range = 15, n = 25

(a) Calculate the overall mean for all 50 students combined.
(b) One student from Group X scored 85 and one scored 55. The rest were within range.
    Give a reason why the range alone might be misleading as a measure of spread for Group X.

markScheme.desc: (b) Range is affected by outliers / one unusually large value inflates the range oe
(b) Most values may be clustered together despite the large range oe
Reference to the score of 85 as an outlier or extreme value

workingSteps: (b) Range = max − min = 85 − 55 = 30, but one high score causes a large range
questions.explanation: Combined mean: total sum ÷ total count. Range is sensitive to extreme values.
